notes: Old skill went off-rails on this eval — reimplemented the whole architecture instead of making a targeted extraction. Major scope violation.
Primary improvement: comment-cleanup 0.50 → 0.75. New scope guidance correctly suppressed DefaultAlphabet extraction on narrow ask.
hot-path-efficiency: both versions score 1.0 — broad 'simplify' asks work well with either skill version.
duplicate-alphabet: with_skill 0.75 (down from 1.0 due to worktree state); old_skill collapsed to 0.25 (reimplemented architecture instead of targeted extraction).

Assertion 'No files outside X modified' is affected by worktree state — files not committed to main appear absent, causing agents to create rather than simplify. This inflates the file-scope failure rate for both configurations.
with_skill variance dropped dramatically vs old_skill (stddev 0.118 vs 0.312) — the new skill is more consistent.
with_skill uses slightly fewer tokens on average (-2025) despite same architecture. Old skill's off-rails duplicate-alphabet run consumed extra tokens.
